relevant_files.summary: Main init orchestration. Writes core Fabric files, installs Claude-specific skill/hook/settings, then runs bootstrap/mcp/hooks sequentially.
Maps clients to bootstrap templates and native target files, causing multi-file external bootstrap artifacts.
Client detection logic. Detects Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, Roo, Gemini, and Codex based on local/home paths.
Claude-only follow-up initialization skill. Indicates Fabric currently models deep init completion through Claude skill continuation.
Current init copy emphasizes sequential stages and a Claude-oriented next step message.
Confirms installed Claude skill/hook/settings and sentinel behavior, showing a complete Claude-only flow exists.
Confirms Codex only has TOML config installation coverage, not skill/hook coverage.
Prior analysis defines fab init as evidence + protocol install, with AI skill taking over for semantic initialization.
Prior analysis already pushes toward reducing duplicated maintenance and making rule sources more structured.

patterns.description: Init runs core file creation first, then bootstrap, MCP config, and hooks in order. Output is post-action summary, not pre-action planning.
Bootstrap writes client-specific prompt files into each client's expected native location.
Claude receives a complete post-init continuation mechanism via skill + hook + settings merge.
Codex is supported for MCP config and bootstrap header insertion, but not for deeper init continuation.

constraints: Current init is intentionally non-destructive by default and aborts on existing key artifacts unless --force is used.
Different AI clients have different native bootstrap entrypoints, so removing all external files may reduce discoverability or compatibility.
Claude continuation flow is already tested and should not be regressed casually.
Codex support already exists at config/bootstrap level, so any redesign should preserve existing TOML integration.

integration_points.description: Best insertion point for interactive planning UI and capability summary because all init stages already pass through here.
Best insertion point for converting full bootstrap templates into thin shims pointing at .fabric-owned docs.
Best insertion point for building a client capability registry instead of only presence detection.
Required for new wizard copy, status labels, and capability matrix copy.

key_findings: The current init flow has stages but lacks a preflight planning/selection layer, which is why it feels thin despite doing many things.
External multi-file bootstrap output is a direct result of per-client native target mapping, not an incidental bug.
Claude has a complete post-init continuation chain; Codex does not, creating an inconsistent multi-client experience.
A .fabric-centered single source of truth is feasible without removing all client-native files, by converting them into thin shims.

_metadata.exploration_scope: fabric init UX, bootstrap artifact layout, skill installation parity
